UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account on the KrishiAI platform, primarily for farmers or cooperative managers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication and communication.

name.description: The full name of the user or farmer.

location.description: The geographic location of the user (e.g., 'Wardha, Maharashtra').

languagePreference.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface and content (e.g., 'en' for English, 'hi' for Hindi).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Farm.title: Farm
Farm.description: Represents a specific farm or plot of land managed by a user, allowing for tracking of agricultural activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Farm entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or manages this farm. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Farm)

name.description: A user-defined name for the farm (e.g., 'North Plot', 'Home Farm').

location.description: Geographic location details of the farm, such as an address or coordinates.

sizeAcres.description: The size of the farm in acres.

soilType.description: The predominant soil type of the farm (e.g., 'Black', 'Alluvial').

lastSoilAnalysisDate.description: The date of the most recent soil analysis performed on the farm.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the farm record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the farm record was last updated.

CropCycle.title: CropCycle
CropCycle.description: Tracks a specific crop cultivation period on a farm, from planting to harvest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CropCycle entity.

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this crop cycle is taking place. (Relationship: Farm 1:N CropCycle)

cropType.description: The type of crop being grown (e.g., 'Cherry Tomatoes', 'Wheat', 'Cotton').

plantingDate.description: The date when the crop was planted.

expectedHarvestDate.description: The anticipated date for harvesting the crop.

status.description: The current stage of the crop cycle (e.g., 'Planned', 'Growing', 'Harvested', 'Completed').

yieldEstimateKg.description: The estimated yield of the crop in kilograms.

actualYieldKg.description: The actual yield of the crop in kilograms after harvest.

carbonCreditsEarned.description: The amount of carbon credits earned for this crop cycle, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the crop cycle record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the crop cycle record was last updated.

DiagnosticReport.title: DiagnosticReport
DiagnosticReport.description: Stores the results and details of an AI-powered disease diagnosis for a specific crop or plant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiagnosticReport entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the diagnosis. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DiagnosticReport)

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this diagnosis was performed. (Relationship: Farm 1:N DiagnosticReport)

cropCycleId.description: Reference to the CropCycle this diagnosis pertains to. (Relationship: CropCycle 1:N DiagnosticReport)

diagnosisDate.description: Timestamp when the diagnosis was performed.

cropType.description: The type of crop for which the diagnosis was made (e.g., 'Tomato', 'Wheat').

issue.description: The diagnosed issue or disease (e.g., 'Early Blight (Alternaria solani)').

confidence.description: The AI's confidence level in the diagnosis, as a percentage (0-100).

severity.description: The severity level of the diagnosed issue (e.g., 'LOW', 'MODERATE', 'HIGH').

remedialAction.description: Recommended remedial action or treatment in English.

remedialActionHindi.description: Recommended remedial action or treatment in Hindi.

imageReferenceUrl.description: URL or reference to the image used for diagnosis, if available.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the diagnostic report record was created.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product available for purchase in the Harit Store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product (e.g., 'Organic Cherry Tomatoes').

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

priceINR.description: The price of the product in Indian Rupees.

currency.description: The currency of the product price.

averageRating.description: The average rating of the product from customer reviews (0-5 scale).

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm from which this product originated. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Product)

clusterName.description: The name of the farming cluster this product belongs to (e.g., 'Wardha Cluster').

isOrganic.description: Indicates whether the product is certified organic.

imageUrl.description: URL for the primary image of the product.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Stores testimonials from farmers, displayed on the KrishiAI marketing website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

authorName.description: The name of the farmer giving the testimonial (English).

authorNameDevanagari.description: The name of the farmer giving the testimonial (Devanagari script).

location.description: The location of the farmer (e.g., 'Wardha, Maharashtra').

quoteEnglish.description: The full testimonial quote in English.

quoteHindi.description: The full testimonial quote in Hindi.

rating.description: The star rating given by the farmer, typically out of 5.

displayOrder.description: An optional numerical value to determine the order in which testimonials are displayed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the testimonial was created/submitted.

isApproved.description: Indicates whether the testimonial has been approved for public display.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. Each document's ID is the Firebase Authentication UID. Ownership is path-based, meaning `{userId}` must match `request.auth.uid` for read/write access. The document includes the `id` field matching the document ID.

Stores details for farms owned by a specific user. This subcollection uses path-based ownership. Each Farm document includes a denormalized `userId` field to match the `{userId}` in its path, ensuring authorization independence for security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile who owns this farm.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific farm.

definition.description: Tracks specific crop cultivation periods for a farm. Each CropCycle document includes denormalized `userId` and `farmId` fields to match their respective parent paths, ensuring authorization independence and simplified ownership checks for security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile who owns the farm and this crop cycle.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Farm where this crop cycle is occurring.

params.name: cropCycleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific crop cycle.

definition.description: Stores AI-powered disease diagnosis reports initiated by a user. Each DiagnosticReport document includes denormalized `userId` and `farmId` fields, as per schema, ensuring authorization independence and simplified ownership checks for security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile who initiated this diagnostic report.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific diagnostic report.

definition.description: Represents products available for purchase in the Harit Store. These documents are publicly readable. For management (create, update, delete), each Product document must include a denormalized `ownerId` (the `userId` of the farm owner) for authorization independence.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific product.

definition.description: Stores testimonials from farmers for public display on the marketing website. Only approved testimonials (`isApproved: true`) are publicly readable. Management (create, update, delete, approve) is typically restricted to administrators or specific roles.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific testimonial.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for KrishiAI prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence via Denormalization and Structural Segregation. 

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
1.  **User-Owned Data (e.g., Farms, CropCycles, DiagnosticReports):** For entities like `Farm`, `CropCycle`, and `DiagnosticReport` which logically belong to a `UserProfile`, the `userId` of the owner is redundantly stored within each document. For instance, a `Farm` document within `/users/{userId}/farms/{farmId}` will explicitly contain a `userId` field matching the `{userId}` in its parent path. Similarly, `CropCycle` documents will contain both `userId` and `farmId`. This denormalization is critical. It eliminates the need for security rules to perform `get()` operations on parent documents (e.g., to fetch the `userId` from a `Farm` when securing a `CropCycle`). Rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`, ensuring atomic operations (transactions, batched writes) are robust and rules are simpler to write and debug.
2.  **Global Entities with Proprietorship (e.g., Products):** The `Product` entity, while globally browsable, is owned and managed by a specific farmer (via their farm). To maintain authorization independence for managing these products, the `Product` document denormalizes the `ownerId` (which is the `userId` of the farm's owner) directly into the `product` document. This allows management rules (e.g., `allow write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId;`) to operate without costly and dependency-creating `get()` calls to the associated `Farm` or `UserProfile` document.

**QAPs (Query as a Permission - How `list` operations are secured):**
1.  **Path-Based Ownership for Private Data:** For user-specific data like `Farm`, `CropCycle`, and `DiagnosticReport`, the structure `/users/{userId}/...` inherently secures `list` operations. Security rules will enforce that `{userId}` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. This means a user can only query and retrieve a list of *their own* farms, crop cycles, or diagnostic reports, preventing accidental or malicious access to other users' private data. This satisfies QAPs as the query itself (e.g., `db.collection('users').doc(myUid).collection('farms').get()`) implicitly contains the authorization context.
2.  **Global Collections with State-Based Visibility:** For `Testimonial` documents, which are publicly viewable on the marketing site, read access is controlled by the `isApproved` field. Rules will permit `list` operations only if `resource.data.isApproved == true`. This allows public listing of approved content without relying on `request.auth.uid` for reads. Write/update operations for testimonials are typically restricted to administrative roles or to the user themselves for submitting unapproved drafts (if `submittedByUserId` were added and matched to `request.auth.uid`).
3.  **Global Collections with Denormalized Proprietor (Public Read, Private Write):** For `Product` documents, `list` operations are open to the public (`allow read: true;`). The authorization rules for write, update, and delete operations will be based on the denormalized `ownerId` within the `Product` document (`allow write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId;`). This design cleanly separates public read access from private management access, fulfilling QAPs for both scenarios.
